Food.exampleSentence: She eats an apple every morning.
Food.hint: A red or green fruit that grows on trees.
A round flat bread topped with cheese and sauce.

Food.exampleSentence: Mom always puts broccoli in our soup.
Food.hint: A dark green vegetable that looks like a tiny tree.

Food.exampleSentence: The children love ice cream on a hot day.
We baked a cake for her birthday.
Food.hint: A sweet baked dessert, often with frosting.

Food.exampleSentence: She made a fresh salad with tomatoes.
Food.hint: A dish made of raw vegetables mixed together.

Food.exampleSentence: My dad loves to cook on weekends.
He picked grapes from the vine.
Food.hint: Small round fruits that grow in clusters.

Animals.exampleSentence: The dog wagged its tail when I came home.
Animals.hint: A loyal pet that barks and loves to play.

Animals.exampleSentence: My cat sleeps on the sofa all day.
Animals.hint: A furry pet that purrs and meows.

Animals.exampleSentence: The elephant used its trunk to drink water.
Animals.hint: The largest land animal, with a long trunk.

Animals.exampleSentence: The lion roared loudly in the jungle.
Animals.hint: A big wild cat called the king of the jungle.

Animals.exampleSentence: We saw a dolphin jumping out of the sea.
Animals.hint: A smart ocean animal that can jump and communicate.

Animals.exampleSentence: A beautiful butterfly landed on the flower.
The turtle slowly crossed the road.
Animals.hint: A slow animal with a hard shell on its back.

Animals.exampleSentence: The parrot can copy everything you say.
Animals.hint: A colorful tropical bird that can talk.

Actions.exampleSentence: She runs to school every morning.
Actions.hint: To move very fast on your feet.

Actions.exampleSentence: They dance together at every school party.
Actions.hint: To move your body to the rhythm of music.

Actions.exampleSentence: She loves to paint pictures of the ocean.
Actions.hint: To put color on a surface using a brush.

Actions.exampleSentence: He reads a new book every week.
Actions.hint: To look at written words and understand them.

Actions.exampleSentence: Children should sleep for at least 8 hours.
Actions.hint: To rest with eyes closed and body inactive.

Actions.exampleSentence: Can you speak a little louder, please?
Actions.hint: To say words out loud using your voice.

Actions.exampleSentence: Please write your name at the top of the paper.
Actions.hint: To form letters and words on a surface.

Actions.exampleSentence: The kids love to jump on the trampoline.
Actions.hint: To push off the ground and go into the air.

School.exampleSentence: The library is full of books on every topic.
School.hint: Written works bound together between covers.

School.exampleSentence: She packed her backpack the night before school.
School.hint: A bag with straps that you carry on your back.

School.exampleSentence: Use a ruler to draw a straight line.
School.hint: A flat tool used to measure length.

School.exampleSentence: School starts at 8 o'clock every morning.
School.hint: A place where students go to learn.

School.exampleSentence: He studied all night to prepare for the test.
School.hint: A set of questions to measure what you know.

School.exampleSentence: The teacher explained the lesson very clearly.
School.hint: A person who helps students learn.

School.exampleSentence: Please use a blue pen to fill out the form.
School.hint: A writing tool that uses ink.

School.exampleSentence: We use a computer in every class now.
School.hint: An electronic machine that processes information.

Travel.exampleSentence: Our flight to London was delayed by two hours.
They lost my luggage at the airport!
Travel.hint: Bags and suitcases you bring on a trip.

Travel.exampleSentence: She planned a detailed itinerary for the whole trip.
Travel.hint: A planned route or schedule for a journey.

Travel.exampleSentence: We need to book our accommodation before we leave.
Travel.hint: A place where you stay when you travel.

Travel.exampleSentence: The officer checked our passports at immigration.
Travel.hint: The process of entering a country from another.

Travel.exampleSentence: The Eiffel Tower is Paris's most famous landmark.
Travel.hint: A famous building or place used to identify a location.

Travel.exampleSentence: Bali is one of the most popular tourist destinations.
Travel.hint: The place you are traveling to.

Travel.exampleSentence: We took a train from Paris to Amsterdam.
Travel.hint: A vehicle that travels on tracks.

Emotions.exampleSentence: She felt frustrated when her computer kept crashing.
Emotions.hint: Feeling upset because something won't work.

Emotions.exampleSentence: The students were fascinated by the science experiment.
Emotions.hint: Extremely interested and attracted by something.

Emotions.exampleSentence: The rainy weather made her feel melancholy.
Emotions.hint: A deep feeling of sadness or sorrow.

Emotions.exampleSentence: He was anxious about the results of his exam.
Emotions.hint: Feeling worried about what might happen.

Emotions.exampleSentence: She was overwhelmed by the amount of homework.
Emotions.hint: Having too much to deal with at once.

Emotions.exampleSentence: He felt vulnerable after sharing his personal story.
Emotions.hint: Feeling open to being hurt emotionally.

Emotions.exampleSentence: After the marathon, the runners were completely exhausted.
Emotions.hint: Extremely tired, having used up all energy.

Emotions.exampleSentence: He was amazed by the magician's tricks.
Emotions.hint: Greatly surprised or filled with wonder.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: A tornado swept through the small town overnight.
Weather & Nature.hint: A violent spinning column of air.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: The lightning lit up the entire sky during the storm.
Weather & Nature.hint: A flash of electricity from a cloud.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: The earthquake triggered a dangerous tsunami.
Weather & Nature.hint: A huge ocean wave caused by an underwater earthquake.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: The volcano erupted and sent ash into the air.
Weather & Nature.hint: A mountain that can shoot hot lava.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: A beautiful rainbow appeared after the rain.
Weather & Nature.hint: An arc of colors in the sky after rain.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: They climbed the mountain over three days.
Weather & Nature.hint: A very large, tall area of land.

Weather & Nature.exampleSentence: The temperature dropped below zero last night.
Weather & Nature.hint: A measure of how hot or cold something is.
All the plants and trees growing in an area.

Sports.exampleSentence: Their team won the national championship.
Sports.hint: The final competition to decide the best team.

Sports.exampleSentence: Weightlifting requires a lot of strength and technique.
Sports.hint: A sport where you lift heavy weights.

Sports.exampleSentence: Swimming laps is great exercise for the whole body.
Sports.hint: Moving through water using arms and legs.

Sports.exampleSentence: Cycling to work saves money and helps the environment.
Sports.hint: Riding a bicycle as sport or transport.

Sports.exampleSentence: Athletes use meditation to stay calm before competitions.
Sports.hint: Quiet mental focus to relax and clear the mind.

Sports.exampleSentence: She trained for six months to run her first marathon.
Sports.hint: A long-distance running race of 42 kilometers.

Sports.exampleSentence: Basketball is popular among middle school students.
Sports.hint: A sport where teams shoot a ball into a hoop.

Sports.exampleSentence: She has been doing gymnastics since she was five.
Sports.hint: A sport involving balance, flexibility and acrobatics.

Technology.exampleSentence: She brought her laptop to the coffee shop to work.
Technology.hint: A portable computer that folds and runs on battery.

Technology.exampleSentence: Almost every student has a smartphone these days.
Technology.hint: A mobile phone that can connect to the internet.

Technology.exampleSentence: Artificial intelligence can now write essays and generate images.
Technology.hint: The ability of computers to perform human-like tasks.

Technology.exampleSentence: GPS uses satellite signals to track your location.
Technology.hint: A man-made object that orbits the Earth.

Technology.exampleSentence: Always use a strong password to protect your accounts.
Technology.hint: A secret word to protect access to something.

Technology.exampleSentence: My phone battery died right in the middle of the call.
Technology.hint: A device that stores electrical energy.

Technology.exampleSentence: The internet connects billions of people worldwide.
Technology.hint: A global network connecting computers around the world.

Technology.exampleSentence: The company stores its data on secure cloud servers.
Technology.hint: Information processed or stored by a computer.

Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.exampleSentence: Don't burn bridges with your old coworkers.
Stop wasting time — time is money!
Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.hint: Time is a valuable resource that shouldn't be wasted.

Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.exampleSentence: Nobody mentioned the elephant in the room.
Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.hint: An obvious problem that everyone ignores.

Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.exampleSentence: He told a joke to break the ice at the party.
Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.hint: To do something to reduce tension in a social situation.

Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.exampleSentence: Her speech gave me a lot of food for thought.
She managed to rise above the criticism.
Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.hint: To not be negatively affected by a bad situation.

Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.word: Hit the Nail on the Head
Idioms & Phrasal Verbs.exampleSentence: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.
She gave him the cold shoulder after the argument.

Careers.exampleSentence: The physician recommended complete bed rest for a week.
Careers.hint: A medical doctor who diagnoses and treats illness.

Careers.exampleSentence: She works as a software engineer at a tech startup.
Careers.hint: A person who designs and builds computer programs.

Careers.exampleSentence: She hired an attorney to help with the legal case.
Careers.hint: A lawyer who represents people in court.

Careers.exampleSentence: The architect designed a stunning modern building.
Careers.hint: A person who designs buildings and structures.

Careers.exampleSentence: The researcher published her findings in a journal.
Careers.hint: A person who systematically investigates a subject.

Careers.exampleSentence: The analyst predicted economic growth next year.
Careers.hint: A person who examines data to find insights.

Careers.exampleSentence: As an educator, she inspired hundreds of students.
Careers.hint: A person whose job is to teach.

Careers.exampleSentence: The chef created a new dish using local ingredients.
Careers.hint: A professional cook who leads a kitchen.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: The activists fought for justice and equal rights.
Abstract Concepts.hint: The quality of being fair and right.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: The two countries signed a treaty to maintain peace.
Abstract Concepts.hint: A state of calm and freedom from conflict.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: Innovation drives economic growth in modern societies.
Abstract Concepts.hint: The introduction of new ideas or methods.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: Globalization has connected economies across the world.
Abstract Concepts.hint: The process of countries becoming interconnected.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: It's a paradox that being too busy makes you less productive.
Abstract Concepts.hint: A statement that seems contradictory but may be true.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: The irony is that the fire station burned down.
Abstract Concepts.hint: When the opposite of what is expected happens.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: The complexity of the problem confused many experts.
Abstract Concepts.hint: The state of having many connected parts.

Abstract Concepts.exampleSentence: Space seems to stretch on toward infinity.
Abstract Concepts.hint: A quantity without end or limit.

Society.exampleSentence: Democracy allows citizens to vote for their leaders.
Society.hint: A system of government where people vote.

Society.exampleSentence: The government used propaganda to spread its message.
Society.hint: Biased information used to promote a cause.

Society.exampleSentence: Diplomacy prevented the conflict from becoming a war.
Society.hint: Managing relations between countries through discussion.

Society.exampleSentence: Sustainability means using resources without harming the future.
Society.hint: Doing things in a way that doesn't damage the environment.
The work of gathering and reporting news.

Society.exampleSentence: Psychology helps us understand human behavior.
Society.hint: The scientific study of the mind and behavior.

Society.exampleSentence: A strong economy provides jobs for the population.
Society.hint: The system of trade and production in a region.

Society.exampleSentence: The legislature passed a new environmental law.
Society.hint: The body of government that makes laws.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: They performed a Shakespeare play at the city theatre.
Arts & Culture.hint: A place or art form where plays are performed.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: Beethoven's Fifth Symphony is one of the most famous pieces.
Arts & Culture.hint: A long piece of music written for an orchestra.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: Impressionism is known for its use of light and loose brushstrokes.
Arts & Culture.hint: An art style focusing on capturing a feeling, not exact details.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: The cinematography in that film was absolutely stunning.
Arts & Culture.hint: The art of shooting and composing scenes in a film.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: She studied classic literature at university.
Arts & Culture.hint: Written works considered to have lasting value.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: The architecture of ancient Rome still influences buildings today.
Arts & Culture.hint: The art and science of designing buildings.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: His powerful rhetoric moved the entire crowd.
Arts & Culture.hint: The art of effective or persuasive speaking.

Arts & Culture.exampleSentence: The Renaissance was a period of great artistic achievement.
Arts & Culture.hint: A period of European cultural rebirth in the 14th–17th centuries.
